mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on standardized communication protocols and security. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential resource overhead for running the platform.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the deployment.

```bash

curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the system PATH.
commands: curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary for your environment.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster's credentials.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys Kagenti to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> This command installs Kagenti in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for Kagenti.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

```

> This command sets up monitoring for the Kagenti deployment.
> This command sets up autoscaling for the Kagenti deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary data. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp kagenti-pod:/path/to/data /local/path/to/backup

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backup your data from the Kagenti pod.
> This command upgrades the Kagenti installation.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kagenti image may not be available or the image tag is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image repository and ensure the correct tag is specified in the Helm chart.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagenti' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU/memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Kagenti deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI agent protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/
gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
